⏺ Here's the full picture on Osmo's interactions with Smiths Detection — pulled from today's IQT meeting transcript,          
  historical Granola notes, and email.
                                                                                                                              
  ---            
  Osmo × Smiths Detection: Summary

  Who is Smiths Detection

  A major airport/border security screening hardware company — one of the leaders in "puff portal" explosive/substance
  detection at airports. ~$1B revenue last year. Competes alongside Leidos and L3Harris in this market. Their hardware is
  deployed broadly but runs static, pre-programmed detection libraries — not AI-updated.

  ---
  Interaction History

  Date: Mar 2025
  Event: Eliot/Rich 1:1
  Key Signal: "Government/IQT deals unlikely to materialize" — pessimism
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Date: Jun 2025
  Event: Eliot/Rich 1:1
  Key Signal: "Government/IQT potential ($20M opportunity)" — renewed interest
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Date: Oct 2025
  Event: Osmo × DARPA meeting
  Key Signal: IQT (Kyle McNulty + AJ Bertone) present; Osmo referenced a prior IQT lab demo; Smiths/defense framing discussed
  ────────────────────────────────────────
  Date: Today (Feb 18, 2026)
  Event: IQT / OSMO Sensors Discussion
  Key Signal: Full strategy session with Kyle McNulty (IQT), Alex, Rich, Hannah Jane, Noah Heller

  ---
  Today's Meeting — Key Takeaways (Feb 18)

  Where Osmo does NOT have an edge:
  - Single-analyte fentanyl detection. Alex was direct: "Smiths, Lidos will always beat us. They focus on that, they have
  pipelines for that." Osmo should stay out of illicit substance detection — "it's not our power alley."
  - Osmo hardware is not 10-100x better than Smiths' — "we're working with the same physics."

  Where Osmo DOES have an edge:
  - Detecting blends (multi-analyte, complex mixtures)
  - AI/software layer on top of existing Smiths/Leidos hardware — Osmo knows their devices are generating far more data than
  they're using, and their systems are "static — not getting smarter in the field"
  - OTA model updates — pushing smarter models to deployed hardware at scale
  - Noise reduction in high-throughput environments (e.g., airport perfume interference in puff portals)

  Proposed path forward:
  - Partnership model: Osmo as AI/software layer on top of Smiths + Leidos hardware. Don't compete on hardware, don't pursue
  primary detection. Focus on software integration for novel detection and model improvement.
  - Alex confirmed he's already had the conversation with Smiths about OTA updates — but said "it hasn't moved very quickly."
  - IQT's recommendation: "Pull the string on the Lidos and Smiths connection — understand their needs. It might be a 24-month
   innovation plan, or it might be a now thing."

  TSA tailwinds IQT flagged:
  - TSA actively trying to fast-track new tech ahead of World Cup + Olympics (US-hosted)
  - Talking to TSA #2 (deputy administrator) — pushing for faster procurement
  - $1B appropriated in the "big beautiful bill" for fentanyl vehicle scanning at borders

  Next steps discussed:
  - Alex + Kyle 1:1 to prep CEO Summit pitch content (tailored to what Osmo can do today vs. aspirational)
  - IQT CEO Summit: May 6–7, Arizona (Alex on the list, invite coming) — 5-min lightning pitch opportunity
  - IQT Showcase: September (date TBD) — booth format, live demos, VIP government meetings
  - Continued relationship-building with TSA, border agencies — even without active pilot

  ---
  Current Status

  No active partnership or contract with Smiths Detection. The relationship is being brokered through IQT. Osmo's angle is
  clear: software + AI on top of their deployed sensor infrastructure, not hardware competition. The conversation exists but
  has been slow-moving. IQT is actively pushing Osmo toward both Smiths and Leidos as strategic go-to-market partners for the
  government/security channel.

  Bottom line: The path is software partnership, not head-on detection competition. Alex has the right instinct. The question
  is who owns moving the Smiths/Leidos conversation forward — worth clarifying ownership (Alex? Brian Hauck given his
  background in this space?).

✻ Cogitated for 3m 21s

⏺ Here's the complete Gmail picture — much richer than the Granola view. There's been an active, substantive technical        
  engagement running since Jan 2025.                                                                                          
                                                 
  ---                                                                                                                         
  Osmo × Smiths Detection: Complete Gmail History                                                                             
                                                                                                                              
  Key Contacts                                                                                                                
                  
  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │                             Smiths                             │                         Osmo                         │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Dr. Alastair Clark — Chief Scientist CBRNE (primary)           │ Brian Hauck — Sensors lead (took over from Rohinton) │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Michael Huxham — secondary                                     │ Harry Pellerin — legal/NDA                           │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Dr. Andrew Hobson — Head of Product Management (added Nov '25) │ Rohinton Mehta — original contact (departed Osmo)    │
  ├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │                                                                │ Rich, Eliot Pence — CC'd                             │
  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  Chronological Timeline

  Jan 10, 2025 — First contact
  Alastair responds to Rohinton Mehta to schedule an intro call. Erica Simons locks in Jan 21 at 10am ET / 3pm UK. This
  relationship started 13+ months ago.

  Feb 2025 — NDA process begins; immediately hits a wall
  Brian takes over from Rohinton. Smiths had been cyberattacked, which knocked out their NDA system for 2 weeks. Alastair
  requested Osmo's company registration info to get legal moving. Harry Pellerin provided: Osmo Labs, PBC, EIN 88-2159047,
  3-year NDA term.

  Mar 27, 2025 — First real milestone: NDA signed + hardware identified
  - ✅ NDA fully executed by both parties
  - 4 Smiths LCD detectors identified and being prepped in their UK labs
  - Undoped sieve packs ordered from production
  - Export licence flagged as required before shipping UK→US — this became the 9-month bottleneck

  Apr–Jul 2025 — Export licence limbo
  Smiths working to get UK export clearance. Brian returns the End User Certificate. Months of waiting.

  Sep 4, 2025 — Brian reopens technical conversation
  While waiting on hardware, Brian proposes using the time to define the technical collaboration:
  - How to enhance Smiths' LCD with Osmo AI/ML
  - Asks about calibration and dynamic detection window adjustment
  - References Adam Saint's XID presentation from IMS conference

  Sep 10, 2025 — Smiths defines performance priorities
  Alastair responds with what Smiths wants Osmo to solve:
  1. False alarm rate reduction — top priority
  2. Expanded detection libraries — incorporating more compounds
  3. Small peak detection — specifically unresolved peaks hidden in larger peaks

  Also clarified: the XID already uses dynamic windows (isoflurane moisture referencing, temp-adjusted Ko ratios) — not
  hard-coded, as Brian suspected.

  Oct–Nov 2025 — Hardware scope narrows
  Alastair kills XID loan idea (no spare units). Pivots to LCD-only focus. Introduces Toxic Industrial Chemicals (TICs) as the
   first test candidate — smart choice because it avoids live agent handling and additional export licensing headaches. TICs
  appear near the RIP (Reactant Ion Peak) and are hard to identify; AI is a natural fit. Dr. Andrew Hobson (Head of Product
  Mgmt) added to thread.

  Nov 17, 2025 — Brian agrees on TIC path
  TICs confirmed as the first experiment. Brian proposes a Dec brainstorming call (never materialized — both sides busy). Asks
   for existing releasable TIC data.

  Jan 12, 2026 — New year check-in
  Brian follows up: any movement on TIC data or export licence?

  Jan 15, 2026 — Alastair: working on it, slow as ever
  "Things don't ever seem to be less busy here 😊." Commits to finding TIC data and progressing export licence.

  Feb 11, 2026 — Brian follows up again
  Still waiting on both items.

  Feb 12, 2026 — 🟢 EXPORT LICENCE CLEARED
  Alastair: "We now have an export licence in place which is good news!"
  - Smiths legal preparing a teaming/loan agreement
  - Alastair will set up a call "in the next week or so" to plan technical details
  - Brian responds: "That sounds great! We look forward to reviewing the document and discussing next steps."

  ---
  Current Status (as of today, Feb 18)

  ┌─────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │          Item           │                                      Status                                      │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ NDA                     │ ✅ Signed (Mar 2025)                                                             │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Export licence (UK→US)  │ ✅ Cleared (Feb 12, 2026)                                                        │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Teaming/loan agreement  │ 🟡 Smiths legal drafting — pending receipt                                       │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ LCD hardware shipment   │ ⏳ Awaiting agreement execution                                                  │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Technical focus         │ TIC detection using Osmo AI/ML on Smiths LCD data                                │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ First experiment scoped │ Small peak / unresolved peak identification + false alarm reduction              │
  ├─────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
  │ Next action             │ Call that Alastair promised to set up — should be imminent (overdue as of today) │
  └─────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

  ---
  Critical Observation

  There's a disconnect between this track and today's IQT conversation. In the IQT meeting, Alex talked about the Smiths
  relationship as vague and slow-moving ("that conversation hasn't moved very quickly"). But there's actually a fully executed
   NDA, specific technical goals defined, an export licence just cleared last week, and a teaming agreement in flight — all
  driven by Brian. Alex may not have the full picture.

  Recommended action: Brief Alex on the actual state of the Brian ↔ Alastair track before any IQT-brokered Smiths outreach.
  You don't want two parallel tracks running without coordination.

✻ Brewed for 1m 40s
